title: Options Trading for Beginners: A Complete Guide
description: Options can hedge your portfolio or blow it up — the difference is understanding the Greeks. A jargon-free guide from calls and puts to real strategies.

schemas.headline: Options Trading for Beginners: A Complete Guide
schemas.description: A clear, jargon-free introduction to options trading covering calls, puts, key terms, strategies, the Greeks, and risk management.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between buying and selling an option?

acceptedAnswer.text: Buying an option gives you the right (but not the obligation) to buy or sell a stock at a specific price. Selling (or writing) an option obligates you to buy or sell the stock if the buyer chooses to exercise their right.

mainEntity.name: Can I lose more money than I invest in options?

acceptedAnswer.text: If you are buying options (long calls or long puts), your maximum loss is strictly limited to the premium you paid. However, if you are selling (writing) 'naked' options, your potential losses can be substantial or theoretically unlimited.

Options have a limited lifespan. Because of a concept called time decay, an option loses value as it approaches its expiration date. If the stock price doesn't move past the option's strike price before expiration, the option will have no value.
